question text: If you were to fully dissolve the bicarbonate of soda in water before pouring it into the vase (instead of leaving it as a powder at the bottom), how would this likely affect the "Bubble Tower" display when the acid is added?

answers.answer_text: The reaction would not happen at all because the base is wet.
answers.text: The reaction would not happen at all because the base is wet.

answers.answer_text: The reaction would occur very rapidly or instantly upon mixing, likely spoiling the slow, rising "lava lamp" bubble effect.
answers.text: The reaction would occur very rapidly or instantly upon mixing, likely spoiling the slow, rising "lava lamp" bubble effect.

answers.answer_text: The bubbles would sink instead of rise because the liquid is too heavy.
answers.text: The bubbles would sink instead of rise because the liquid is too heavy.

answers.answer_text: The oil would turn solid immediately upon contact with the dissolved base.
answers.text: The oil would turn solid immediately upon contact with the dissolved base.

answers.answer_text: The solution would turn yellow instead of pink or blue-green.
answers.text: The solution would turn yellow instead of pink or blue-green.

answers.answer_text: The vinegar would float on top of the oil and never reach the dissolved base.
answers.text: The vinegar would float on top of the oil and never reach the dissolved base.

a: The reaction would not happen at all because the base is wet.
b: The reaction would occur very rapidly or instantly upon mixing, likely spoiling the slow, rising "lava lamp" bubble effect.
c: The bubbles would sink instead of rise because the liquid is too heavy.
d: The oil would turn solid immediately upon contact with the dissolved base.
e: The solution would turn yellow instead of pink or blue-green.
